title: fix: restore Klein RunPod build path
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Restores `image.pollinations.ai/klein-runpod` as the stable Klein deployment directory.
- Repairs the existing Klein container workflow by restoring its expected Docker build context.
- Keeps the Vast provisioning script and operational guide aligned with the restored path.
- Leaves the model registry provider as `vast` and does not change production routing or pricing.

Root cause: the Vast migration renamed the established directory without updating the hidden GitHub Actions workflow, causing the post-merge container build to fail because its context no longer existed.

Validation:
- `bash -n image.pollinations.ai/klein-runpod/setup-vast.sh`
- `python3 -m py_compile image.pollinations.ai/klein-runpod/handler.py`
- `git diff --check`
- Confirmed the workflow context, Dockerfile, handler, and requirements files exist

gist.headline: Klein found its way home
gist.blurb: The Klein container has its old nest back. Restoring the expected build path lets the post-merge image workflow hatch containers again.
gist.summary: Restored `image.pollinations.ai/klein-runpod` as Klein’s deployment directory so the hidden GitHub Actions container build can again find its Docker context, handler, requirements, and Vast setup script.
gist.impact: Klein deployments can build reliably again without changing the `vast` model provider, production routing, or pricing.
gist.visual_concept: Depict Klein’s deployment files as a cozy little container workshop being returned to its clearly labeled `klein-runpod` garden shed. Polly, wearing a tiny tool belt, guides a chunky Docker crate along a path back to the shed while a CRT monitor robot verifies the restored build context; include code-plants and a subtle Vast-themed cloud or provisioning clipboard.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly, the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot with pink blush, light blue wings, and a tiny tool belt, guiding a chunky Docker crate back into a garden shed labeled `klein-runpod`; a helpful CRT monitor robot checks a glowing build checklist beside code-plants and small flowers. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel gradient background, dark purple (#110518) outlines, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes like Stardew Valley or A Short Hike, nostalgic and emotionally warm.
